ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e

The legacy `*Store.MigrateToAxon` method (now exposed via `Migrator.MigrateToAxon` in MIG bead, returning `ErrDeprecated`) writes JSONL files under `.ddx/axon/` rather than performing an actual JSONL→Postgres migration. Per the audit in `docs/plans/plan-2026-05-10-axon-only-architecture.md`, production AxonStore deployment requires migrating existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl` corpora into Lakebase Postgres. The real importer is unimplemented.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/migrate.go` (function previously at `*Store.MigrateToAxon`, now folded into `*storeMigrator` per MIG bead) writes JSONL into a sidecar directory. The function does not connect to Postgres or invoke `AxonStore.Apply`/`AxonStore.Create`. Production deployment cannot use it to populate Lakebase.

# PROPOSED FIX

1. Implement a real importer in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/import_postgres.go` (or equivalent post-BL location). The importer:
   - Reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (and `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` if present) — uses existing JSONL parser.
   - For each bead, calls `axonStore.Create(ctx, &b)` to insert into Postgres. Idempotent on `ErrConflict` (skip + log).
   - For each bead, appends events via `axonStore.Apply(ctx, id, AppendEventOp{...})` per TD-027 §13 vocabulary (events come from `Extra["events"]` on the JSONL bead or from sidecar `.ddx/attachments/<bead>/events.jsonl`).
   - Tracks progress, supports `--dry-run` (counts without writes), `--limit N` (batched migration).
   - Verifies post-migration: read back beads from Postgres, assert field-for-field equality with source JSONL (sample-based for large corpora).

2. Expose via `Migrator` interface (added in MIG): rename/replace `MigrateToAxon` with the real implementation; same method name, different (correct) behavior.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` to support the importer with appropriate flags:
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --dry-run`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --apply [--limit N]`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --verify` (read-back + checksum)

4. Remove the deprecated JSONL-writing behavior. `ErrDeprecated` return from MIG bead is replaced by the real implementation.

# NON-SCOPE
- Schema-versioning migration ladder (separate concern; deferred until first v1→v2 breaking change).
- Real-wire integration test against actual Postgres — separate WIRE bead.
- Continuous sync between JSONL and Postgres — not in scope; this is one-shot migration.
- AxonStore itself — implemented in AX (`ddx-9c5bca8f`).

# Acceptance criteria

1. New file `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/import_postgres.go` (or equivalent) implements the JSONL→Postgres importer. Uses `axonStore.Create` + `axonStore.Apply` for inserts + event appends.
2. Existing `Migrator.MigrateToAxon` method returns the real importer's result (not `ErrDeprecated`).
3. CLI flags wired: `--to-axon --dry-run`, `--to-axon --apply`, `--to-axon --verify`. Test: `TestImporter_DryRun_CountsOnly`, `TestImporter_Apply_WritesToPostgres`, `TestImporter_Verify_DetectsDrift`.
4. Idempotency: re-running `--apply` on an already-imported corpus is a no-op (skips conflicts). Test: `TestImporter_Apply_Idempotent_OnReRun`.
5. Field-fidelity: every JSONL bead field is preserved in Postgres, including `extra` preserved-keys. Test: `TestImporter_PreservesExtras`.
6. Event ordering: events imported preserve monotonic `created_at` (TD-027 §11.2 invariant 8). Test: `TestImporter_PreservesEventOrdering`.
7. Attachment migration: sidecar files under `.ddx/attachments/<bead>/` are uploaded to Unity Catalog Volumes (or local-equivalent for testing). Test: `TestImporter_MigratesAttachments`.
8. Verification mode: post-import, sample N beads, read back from AxonStore, assert equality with source. Test: `TestImporter_Verify_RoundTripPassesOnCleanImport`.
9. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green (with httptest-served Axon-shaped GraphQL endpoint or in-memory backend).
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

The conformance suite (`ddx-958b8fc3`) parameterizes tests across `*Store` and `AxonStore`, but `AxonStore` is tested against an httptest-served Axon-shaped GraphQL endpoint — a mock. Production AxonStore deployment requires verifying behavior against an actual Postgres-backed Axon service. Without real-wire tests, semantic differences (timestamp precision, transaction isolation, NULL handling, etc.) can silently diverge between the mock and prod.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/axon/` and the conformance suite use mocked transports. No CI job runs AxonStore against real Postgres. Per `docs/plans/plan-2026-05-10-axon-only-architecture.md`'s audit, "Zero tests run against Postgres" was a gap. The current state remains gap-ful for production verification.

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a real-wire integration test package under `cli/internal/bead/integration/axon/`. Each test:
   - Provisions a Postgres instance (via Docker testcontainer, `dockertest`, or similar in-CI Postgres).
   - Starts an Axon service against that Postgres (or uses Axon's standalone test mode).
   - Constructs `AxonStore` via the real GraphQL transport pointing at the Axon service.
   - Runs the existing conformance suite + Axon-specific tests against the real backend.

2. Test cases (in addition to the parameterized conformance):
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_ClaimCAS_AtomicUnderConcurrency` — 10 concurrent claims; assert exactly one succeeds, others get `ErrAlreadyClaimed`.
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_HeartbeatNativeUpdate` — assert `SetClaimHeartbeat` produces a single `UPDATE` SQL statement (no full-row read-modify-write); verify via Postgres query logs.
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_EventAppend_Monotonic` — concurrent event appends preserve TD-027 §11.2 invariant 8 (monotonic per bead).
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_TransactionIsolation` — `Apply` operations are atomic under SERIALIZABLE / READ COMMITTED (whatever Lakebase default is).
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_NullPreservation` — `omitempty` JSONL fields map correctly to Postgres NULL/JSONB representation.
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_TimestampPrecision` — RFC3339Nano roundtrips with Postgres `TIMESTAMPTZ` (nanosecond truncation behavior documented).
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_SchemaVersionDefault` — beads with `schema_version` absent default to v1 on read (TD-027 §11.4).

3. CI job: `.github/workflows/integration-axon.yml` runs the real-wire tests on a Postgres service container. Gated to manual trigger initially (Postgres setup is slow); converted to nightly once stable. Not on every PR.

4. Documentation: `cli/internal/bead/integration/axon/README.md` explains how to run locally (`docker-compose up postgres-test` + `go test -tags=integration ./internal/bead/integration/axon/...`).

# NON-SCOPE
- Production Lakebase setup — separate ops/infra concern.
- Performance benchmarks — separate work; this bead is correctness.
- Migrating the existing conformance suite to use real Postgres — would slow every PR. The httptest-served suite stays as the fast inner-loop test; real-wire is a gated outer-loop verification.

# Acceptance criteria

1. New package `cli/internal/bead/integration/axon/` with at least 7 real-wire tests listed above.
2. Tests run against a real Postgres instance (testcontainer or service container in CI). Test infrastructure documented in package README.
3. Build tag `//go:build integration` separates these tests from the default test run. `go test ./...` (no tag) doesn't run them; `go test -tags=integration ./internal/bead/integration/axon/...` does.
4. CI workflow `.github/workflows/integration-axon.yml` provisioned. Initial trigger: `workflow_dispatch` (manual). Validation: at least one successful run end-to-end before merge.
5. The full parameterized conformance suite from `ddx-958b8fc3` runs against the real Postgres backend (re-using the same test cases). Mock vs. real-wire produces identical pass/fail signal for every test.
6. Real-wire-specific findings documented as new tests (concurrency CAS, native heartbeat update via Postgres logs, transaction isolation, NULL/JSONB handling, timestamp precision, schema_version defaults).
7. Docs link added to TD-027 §28.4 (downstream acceptance) once landed.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel errors.
4. `cli/internal/bead/context.go` — typed accessors `WithIdentity`/`IdentityFromContext`/`WithTrace`/`TraceFromContext`. Package docstring per TD-027 §9 enumerating allowed ctx values.
5. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` (expanded) — declare `BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle` (Create + Apply only at this stage), `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter` interfaces. All ctx-first. Event interfaces are declarations only in this bead; no `*Store` event compile-time assertions until E. Composite `Backend` is not added yet (other sub-interfaces missing until per-concern beads land). `OperationApplier` optional interface declared. `RawBackend` retained with new docstring warning new backends off whole-corpus shape.

## Modify *Store

6. Add `*Store.Apply(ctx, id, op Operation) error` method per TD-027 §7.2: type-asserts wrapped `RawBackend` to `OperationApplier` and delegates; otherwise generic `WithLock + ReadAll + op.Apply + WriteAll`.
7. Add ctx as first parameter to `*Store.Init`, `ReadAll`, `ReadAllFiltered`, `Get`, `GetWithArchive`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, plus `GenID`. Do not change `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind` here; E owns event ctx threading.

## Modify JSONLBackend

8. Add `JSONLBackend.Apply(ctx, id, op Operation) error` — satisfies `OperationApplier`. Empty type-switch + generic fallback (the per-concern Claim bead adds the `SetClaimHeartbeat`/`ClearClaimHeartbeat` sidecar-optimization cases later).

## Caller migration (ctx threading only — scoped to foundational non-event methods)

9. Update every caller in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that calls the foundational non-event methods (Init/ReadAll/Get/Create/Update/Close/GenID) to pass `ctx` through. Use `cmd.Context()` for cobra commands; existing request ctx for HTTP/GraphQL handlers; `context.Background()` only where no ctx is naturally available.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:356:{"created_at":"2026-05-11T19:58:56.47101434Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-e1c743d3","depends_on_id":"ddx-c6317784","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:58:56Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 claim/heartbeat methods (`Claim`, `ClaimWithOptions`, `Unclaim`, `Heartbeat`, `TouchClaimHeartbeat`, `RemoveClaimHeartbeat`, `ClaimHeartbeatFresh`). Workers in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go`, `cli/internal/server/workers.go`, and the bead CLI hold `*Store` concretely for these. Per TD-027 §12 + §14, claim semantics belong in `ops/claim/` helper + typed `Operation` values, with the storage primitive being `Apply(ctx, id, ClaimOp{...})`. JSONLBackend's sidecar-write optimization for heartbeats (per `cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go`) MUST be preserved through the optional `OperationApplier` interface added in Foundation.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes the 7 claim/heartbeat methods. Heartbeats are written via a sidecar (`claim_liveness.go`) to avoid corpus churn at high frequency. The Foundation bead added `*Store.Apply` + `JSONLBackend.Apply` with an empty type-switch + generic fallback. The sidecar optimization needs the `SetClaimHeartbeat` case wired into `JSONLBackend.Apply`'s switch.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §11.5 + §12 + §14 for the claim concern:

1. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`:
   - `ClaimOp{Owner, Session, Worktree string}` — CAS via `Apply` error return; sets claim fields and `claim.at = time.Now()`. Returns `ErrAlreadyClaimed` from `Apply(*Bead)` if claim is held by different owner.
   - `UnclaimOp{RequireOwner string}` — clears claim; returns `ErrNotClaimedByOwner` if RequireOwner non-empty and doesn't match.
   - `SetClaimHeartbeat{At time.Time}`, `ClearClaimHeartbeat{}` — heartbeat field mutators.
2. Wire `JSONLBackend.Apply` switch cases for `SetClaimHeartbeat` and `ClearClaimHeartbeat` to use `writeClaimSidecar` / `removeClaimSidecar` (preserving today's optimization). All other ops fall through to generic load-mutate-save.
3. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/claim/liveness.go`:
   - `Acquire(ctx, l BeadLifecycle, id string, opts AcquireOptions) error` — wraps `ClaimOp`.
   - `Release(ctx, l, id string) error` — wraps `UnclaimOp{}` (unconditional).
   - `ReleaseAs(ctx, l, id, requiredOwner string) error` — wraps `UnclaimOp{RequireOwner: requiredOwner}`.
   - `Touch(ctx, l, id string) error` — wraps `SetClaimHeartbeat{At: time.Now()}`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id string) error` — wraps `ClearClaimHeartbeat{}`.
   - `IsFresh(b *Bead, maxAge time.Duration) bool` — pure predicate.
4. Migrate callers from `*Store.Claim`/`ClaimWithOptions`/`Unclaim`/`Heartbeat`/`TouchClaimHeartbeat`/`RemoveClaimHeartbeat`/`ClaimHeartbeatFresh` to `ops/claim/` helpers:
   - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go` (worker claim/heartbeat path)
   - `cli/internal/server/workers.go`
   - `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go`
   - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (operator `ddx bead update --claim`/`--unclaim`)
   - `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go`, related agent files
5. Remove `*Store.Claim`, `ClaimWithOptions`, `Unclaim`, `Heartbeat`, `TouchClaimHeartbeat`, `RemoveClaimHeartbeat`, `ClaimHeartbeatFresh` (7 methods).

# NON-SCOPE
- Stale-claim sweep policy — TD-031 §6.2 owns that operational concern; storage exposes the field and helpers; sweep policy lives in the drain loop.
- Other concerns (lifecycle, cancel, cooldown, etc.) — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — bead `ddx-74452926`.
- Modifying claim semantics (TD-027 §12).

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares the 4 claim Operation types. `ClaimOp.Apply` returns `ErrAlreadyClaimed` on owner mismatch. `UnclaimOp.Apply` returns `ErrNotClaimedByOwner` when `RequireOwner` is set and doesn't match. Tests: `TestOperation_ClaimOp_RejectsConflict`, `TestOperation_UnclaimOp_RequireOwner_Honored`, `TestOperation_UnclaimOp_RequireOwner_Empty_Unconditional`.
2. `JSONLBackend.Apply` switch cases for `SetClaimHeartbeat`/`ClearClaimHeartbeat` write only the sidecar; main corpus untouched. Test: `TestJSONLBackend_Apply_SetClaimHeartbeat_UsesSidecar` (compare mtime of `beads.jsonl` before/after).
3. `cli/internal/bead/ops/claim/` package exists with the helpers + tests: `TestOpsClaim_Acquire_HappyPath`, `TestOpsClaim_Acquire_ReturnsErrAlreadyClaimed`, `TestOpsClaim_Release_HappyPath`, `TestOpsClaim_ReleaseAs_RequiresMatchingOwner`, `TestOpsClaim_Touch_BumpsHeartbeat`, `TestOpsClaim_IsFresh_RespectsMaxAge`.
4. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 claim/heartbeat methods. Verified by grep.
5. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store` outside the bead package. Verified by grep.
6. Workers continue to claim, heartbeat, and unclaim beads correctly. Test: end-to-end smoke `TestWorkerLifecycle_ClaimHeartbeatUnclaim` exercising a fake worker through `ops/claim/` helpers.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
